rag_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in the component type scope
Place implementation annexes in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope in AGREE expressions
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL structure when modifying the model
Use only identifiers that are visible in the current scope
Preserve the original component hierarchy and connections
Place annexes in the appropriate scope based on their purpose
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not place annexes in inappropriate scopes
Do not use identifiers that are not visible in the current scope
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
